title: Phlegmariurus pichianus
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus pichianus is a species of plant in the family Lycopodiaceae, commonly known as club mosses. This plant is native to Central and South America, and its classification has been subject to recent revisions. The genus Phlegmariurus was previously included in the genus Huperzia, but it has since been reclassified. As a member of the Lycopodiaceae family, Phlegmariurus pichianus is a type of lycopod, a group of vascular plants that are thought to have evolved over 360 million years ago. The classification of Phlegmariurus pichianus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
